Let me read the files directly:

Contents of file engine/ctgov_history.py:

1 | """Sponsor forecast revisions, reconstructed from ClinicalTrials.gov version history.
2 | 
3 | Registered trials carry a primary completion date (PCD). Sponsors may revise it at
4 | any time, and every revision is kept as a numbered protocol version. Nobody diffs
5 | them, so the revision path is a free, timestamped record of how a sponsor's own
6 | forecast moved -- and of how long it held a date it was going to miss.
7 | 
8 | Two endpoints, neither in the public v2 docs, both unauthenticated:
9 | 
10 |     /api/int/studies/{nct}/history          -> every version, with a change date
11 |     /api/int/studies/{nct}/history/{n}      -> the full protocol snapshot at version n
12 | 
13 | The listing is one request. Snapshots are one request each, so a 95-version trial is
14 | 95 requests if fetched naively. Two things keep that bounded: `moduleLabels` names
15 | which modules changed in each version, and PCD lives in the status module, so any
16 | version that did not touch it cannot have moved the date. Snapshots are cached on
17 | disk, keyed by (nct, version), because history is immutable once written.
18 | 
19 | The derived quantity that matters is not the total slip. It is `held_days`: how close
20 | the then-current date was when the sponsor finally moved it. A sponsor that pushes a
21 | date out eighteen months in advance is forecasting. One that moves it eleven days
22 | before it arrives was holding a date it had stopped believing.
23 | """
24 | from __future__ import annotations
25 | 
26 | import json
27 | import os
28 | import subprocess
29 | import time
30 | from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict
31 | from datetime import date, datetime
32 | 
33 | BASE = "https://clinicaltrials.gov/api/int/studies"
34 | # SEC and NIH both ask for a contact in the UA. Same courtesy here.
35 | UA = os.environ.get("CTGOV_UA", "catalyst-integrity-desk (kris10harim@gmail.com)")
36 | CACHE = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(__file__)), "data", "cache")
37 | SLEEP = float(os.environ.get("CTGOV_SLEEP", "0.12"))  # ~8 req/s, well inside tolerance
38 | 
39 | # The module that carries primaryCompletionDateStruct. A version whose moduleLabels
40 | # omits this cannot have moved the date, so its snapshot is never fetched.
41 | STATUS_LABEL = "Study Status"
42 | 
43 | 
44 | def _get(url: str, retries: int = 3) -> dict:
45 |     """Fetch JSON via curl.
46 | 
47 |     Not urllib, and this is not a style choice. The endpoint is gated on TLS
48 |     fingerprint, not on headers: every urllib request returns 403 while the identical
49 |     request through curl returns 200, including with a browser User-Agent, an explicit
50 |     Accept, and gzip encoding. Tested four header combinations against a curl control.
51 |     So `requests` will fail here too -- it shares OpenSSL's fingerprint. The options are
52 |     shelling out to curl or adding curl_cffi; curl ships with macOS and needs no
53 |     dependency, so the crawler shells out.
54 |     """
55 |     for attempt in range(retries):
56 |         p = subprocess.run(
57 |             ["curl", "-sS", "--fail", "--max-time", "30", "-H", f"User-Agent: {UA}", url],
58 |             capture_output=True, text=True,
59 |         )
60 |         if p.returncode == 0:
61 |             try:
62 |                 return json.loads(p.stdout)
63 |             except json.JSONDecodeError:
64 |                 pass  # truncated body, worth one more try
65 |         if attempt == retries - 1:
66 |             raise RuntimeError(f"GET {url} failed: {p.stderr.strip() or p.returncode}")
67 |         time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
68 |     raise RuntimeError("unreachable")
69 | 
70 | 
71 | def _cached(nct: str, version: int) -> dict:
72 |     os.makedirs(CACHE, exist_ok=True)
73 |     path = os.path.join(CACHE, f"{nct}-v{version}.json")
74 |     if os.path.exists(path):
75 |         with open(path) as f:
76 |             return json.load(f)
77 |     time.sleep(SLEEP)
78 |     d = _get(f"{BASE}/{nct}/history/{version}")
79 |     with open(path, "w") as f:
80 |         json.dump(d, f)
81 |     return d
82 | 
83 | 
84 | def _parse_date(s: str | None) -> date | None:
85 |     """Registry dates come as YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM. Month-only means the sponsor
86 |     did not commit to a day; treat it as the first, and do not pretend otherwise."""
87 |     if not s:
88 |         return None
89 |     for fmt in ("%Y-%m-%d", "%Y-%m"):
90 |         try:
91 |             return datetime.strptime(s, fmt).date()
92 |         except ValueError:
93 |             continue
94 |     return None
95 | 
96 | 
97 | @dataclass
98 | class Revision:
99 |     """One version at which the registered primary completion date changed."""
100 |     version: int
101 |     submitted: str          # when the sponsor filed this revision
102 |     pcd: str                # the date as of this version
103 |     pcd_type: str           # ESTIMATED or ACTUAL
104 |     status: str
105 |     moved_days: int | None  # how far the date moved vs the previous version
106 |     held_days: int | None   # days remaining on the OLD date when it was moved
107 | 
108 |     @property
109 |     def is_late_move(self) -> bool:
110 |         """Moved with under 90 days left on a date the sponsor had been showing.
111 |         Not proof of anything on its own; it is the observation the study counts."""
112 |         return self.held_days is not None and 0 <= self.held_days < 90
113 | 
114 |     @property
115 |     def carried_expired(self) -> bool:
116 |         """The date being replaced had ALREADY PASSED when the sponsor replaced it.
117 | 
118 |         A stronger signal than a late move, and a different one. A late revision can
119 |         be a genuine surprise late in a trial. A date left standing after it lapsed
120 |         means the public registry showed a completion date the sponsor had already
121 |         missed, for `-held_days` days, with no correction. Rocket Pharmaceuticals
122 |         carried a 2022-06 date until 2024-04 -- 677 days expired.
123 |         """
124 |         return self.held_days is not None and self.held_days < 0
125 | 
126 |     @property
127 |     def days_expired(self) -> int:
128 |         return -self.held_days if self.carried_expired else 0
129 | 
130 | 
131 | @dataclass
132 | class TrialHistory:
133 |     nct: str
134 |     sponsor: str
135 |     phases: list[str]
136 |     n_versions: int
137 |     revisions: list[Revision]
138 | 
139 |     @property
140 |     def first_pcd(self) -> str | None:
141 |         return self.revisions[0].pcd if self.revisions else None
142 | 
143 |     @property
144 |     def last_pcd(self) -> str | None:
145 |         return self.revisions[-1].pcd if self.revisions else None
146 | 
147 |     @property
148 |     def total_slip_days(self) -> int | None:
149 |         a, b = _parse_date(self.first_pcd), _parse_date(self.last_pcd)
150 |         return (b - a).days if a and b else None
151 | 
152 |     @property
153 |     def n_late_moves(self) -> int:
154 |         return sum(r.is_late_move for r in self.revisions)
155 | 
156 |     @property
157 |     def n_expired_carried(self) -> int:
158 |         return sum(r.carried_expired for r in self.revisions)
159 | 
160 |     @property
161 |     def max_days_expired(self) -> int:
162 |         """Longest stretch this trial showed a completion date that had already passed."""
163 |         return max((r.days_expired for r in self.revisions), default=0)
164 | 
165 |     def as_dict(self) -> dict:
166 |         d = asdict(self)
167 |         d.update(first_pcd=self.first_pcd, last_pcd=self.last_pcd,
168 |                  total_slip_days=self.total_slip_days, n_late_moves=self.n_late_moves)
169 |         return d
170 | 
171 | 
172 | def fetch_history(nct: str) -> TrialHistory:
173 |     """Walk one trial's version history and return only the versions that moved the
174 |     primary completion date."""
175 |     listing = _get(f"{BASE}/{nct}/history")
176 |     changes = listing.get("changes", [])
177 |     if not changes:
178 |         raise ValueError(f"{nct}: no version history returned")
179 | 
180 |     # Version 0 is always fetched (it establishes the first date); after that only
181 |     # versions that touched the status module can have moved it.
182 |     candidates = [c for c in changes
183 |                   if c["version"] == 0 or STATUS_LABEL in (c.get("moduleLabels") or [])]
184 | 
185 |     sponsor, phases = "", []
186 |     revisions: list[Revision] = []
187 |     prev_pcd: date | None = None
188 |     prev_raw = ""
189 | 
190 |     for c in candidates:
191 |         snap = _cached(nct, c["version"])
192 |         proto = snap.get("study", snap).get("protocolSection", {})
193 |         status_mod = proto.get("statusModule", {})
194 |         pcd_struct = status_mod.get("primaryCompletionDateStruct") or {}
195 |         raw = pcd_struct.get("date")
196 |         pcd = _parse_date(raw)
197 |         if pcd is None:
198 |             continue
199 | 
200 |         if not sponsor:
201 |             sponsor = (proto.get("sponsorCollaboratorsModule", {})
202 |                        .get("leadSponsor", {}).get("name", ""))
203 |             phases = proto.get("designModule", {}).get("phases", []) or []
204 | 
205 |         if prev_pcd is not None and pcd == prev_pcd:
206 |             continue  # status module changed for some other reason
207 | 
208 |         submitted = _parse_date(c.get("lastUpdateSubmitQcDate") or c.get("date"))
209 |         moved = (pcd - prev_pcd).days if prev_pcd else None
210 |         # How much runway the OLD date still had when the sponsor moved it. Negative
211 |         # means they moved it after it had already passed, which is its own signal.
212 |         held = (prev_pcd - submitted).days if prev_pcd and submitted else None
213 | 
214 |         revisions.append(Revision(
215 |             version=c["version"],
216 |             submitted=submitted.isoformat() if submitted else "",
217 |             pcd=raw,
218 |             pcd_type=pcd_struct.get("type", ""),
219 |             status=c.get("status", ""),
220 |             moved_days=moved,
221 |             held_days=held,
222 |         ))
223 |         prev_pcd, prev_raw = pcd, raw
224 | 
225 |     return TrialHistory(nct=nct, sponsor=sponsor, phases=phases,
226 |                         n_versions=len(changes), revisions=revisions)
227 | 
228 | 
229 | def demo() -> None:
230 |     """Self-check against a trial whose history is known to be long and messy."""
231 |     h = fetch_history("NCT04613596")
232 |     assert h.n_versions > 50, h.n_versions
233 |     assert h.sponsor, "lead sponsor should resolve"
234 |     assert len(h.revisions) >= 4, f"expected several PCD moves, got {len(h.revisions)}"
235 |     # First recorded date must precede the last, or the walk is out of order.
236 |     assert h.revisions == sorted(h.revisions, key=lambda r: r.version)
237 |     # moved_days is None only on the first revision.
238 |     assert all(r.moved_days is not None for r in h.revisions[1:])
239 | 
240 |     print(f"{h.nct}  {h.sponsor}  {'/'.join(h.phases) or 'n/a'}")
241 |     print(f"  {h.n_versions} versions, {len(h.revisions)} of them moved the date")
242 |     print(f"  {h.first_pcd} -> {h.last_pcd}   total slip {h.total_slip_days} days")
243 |     print(f"  late moves (<90d notice): {h.n_late_moves}")
244 |     print()
245 |     print(f"  {'ver':>4} {'submitted':>11} {'PCD':>11} {'type':>10} {'moved':>7} {'held':>6}")
246 |     for r in h.revisions:
247 |         print(f"  {r.version:>4} {r.submitted:>11} {r.pcd:>11} {r.pcd_type:>10} "
248 |               f"{'' if r.moved_days is None else f'{r.moved_days:+d}':>7} "
249 |               f"{'' if r.held_days is None else r.held_days:>6}"
250 |               f"{'  <- late' if r.is_late_move else ''}")
251 |     print("\nok")
252 | 
253 | 
254 | if __name__ == "__main__":
255 |     demo()
256 |

Contents of file engine/gap.py:

1 | """The catalyst contract: can this company fund itself to its own next readout?
2 | 
3 |     funding gap = runway exhaustion date - registered primary completion date
4 | 
5 | Negative means the money runs out first. Both sides are deterministic and sourced:
6 | the left from SEC XBRL tags, the right from the sponsor's own registry filing. No
7 | model contributes a number to either.
8 | 
9 | What makes this more than a subtraction is the third column. The registry date is not
10 | an observation, it is a *claim the sponsor can revise at will*, and every revision is
11 | timestamped. So alongside the gap we carry the sponsor's revision behaviour: how many
12 | times the date moved, and how much notice was given each time. A company that pushes a
13 | readout eighteen months out with a year of warning is forecasting. One that moves it
14 | three weeks before it arrives was showing a date it had stopped believing -- and if
15 | that behaviour turns out to cluster in cash-constrained sponsors, the registry is not
16 | a data source, it is a disclosure channel with incentives.
17 | 
18 | That last claim is a hypothesis, not a result. This module assembles the panel needed
19 | to test it. It does not test it, and nothing here should be presented as if it had.
20 | """
21 | from __future__ import annotations
22 | 
23 | import json
24 | import urllib.parse
25 | from dataclasses import dataclass
26 | from datetime import date
27 | 
28 | from engine.ctgov_history import TrialHistory, _get, _parse_date, fetch_history
29 | from engine.runway import Runway, compute_runway, ticker_to_cik
30 | 
31 | V2 = "https://clinicaltrials.gov/api/v2/studies"
32 | # Phase 2/3 only. Early-phase trials are not value-inflection points, and including
33 | # them buries the catalyst in a pile of dose-escalation studies.
34 | PIVOTAL_PHASES = {"PHASE2", "PHASE3", "PHASE2_PHASE3"}
35 | LIVE = {"RECRUITING", "ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING", "ENROLLING_BY_INVITATION", "NOT_YET_RECRUITING"}
36 | 
37 | 
38 | def find_trials(sponsor: str, limit: int = 40) -> list[dict]:
39 |     """Live pivotal-ish trials for one lead sponsor.
40 | 
41 |     The sponsor field is free text with no CIK, so this join is the engineering tax
42 |     of the whole project: matching is by name, and names drift (subsidiaries, "Inc."
43 |     vs "Inc", post-merger renames). Good enough to demo one company; a universe run
44 |     needs an alias table and hand review of the largest names.
45 |     """
46 |     q = urllib.parse.urlencode({
47 |         "query.spons": sponsor,
48 |         "fields": ",".join([
49 |             "protocolSection.identificationModule.nctId",
50 |             "protocolSection.identificationModule.briefTitle",
51 |             "protocolSection.statusModule.overallStatus",
52 |             "protocolSection.statusModule.primaryCompletionDateStruct",
53 |             "protocolSection.designModule.phases",
54 |             "protocolSection.sponsorCollaboratorsModule.leadSponsor",
55 |         ]),
56 |         "pageSize": limit,
57 |     })
58 |     out = []
59 |     for s in _get(f"{V2}?{q}").get("studies", []):
60 |         p = s.get("protocolSection", {})
61 |         phases = set(p.get("designModule", {}).get("phases", []) or [])
62 |         status = p.get("statusModule", {}).get("overallStatus", "")
63 |         pcd = p.get("statusModule", {}).get("primaryCompletionDateStruct", {}) or {}
64 |         if not (phases & PIVOTAL_PHASES) or status not in LIVE or not pcd.get("date"):
65 |             continue
66 |         out.append({
67 |             "nct": p["identificationModule"]["nctId"],
68 |             "title": p["identificationModule"].get("briefTitle", ""),
69 |             "status": status,
70 |             "pcd": pcd["date"],
71 |             "pcd_type": pcd.get("type", ""),
72 |             "phases": sorted(phases),
73 |             "sponsor": p.get("sponsorCollaboratorsModule", {}).get("leadSponsor", {}).get("name", ""),
74 |         })
75 |     return sorted(out, key=lambda t: t["pcd"])
76 | 
77 | 
78 | @dataclass
79 | class CatalystContract:
80 |     """One testable claim: this company reaches this readout on this money."""
81 |     runway: Runway
82 |     trial: dict
83 |     history: TrialHistory | None = None
84 | 
85 |     @property
86 |     def catalyst_date(self) -> date | None:
87 |         return _parse_date(self.trial["pcd"])
88 | 
89 |     @property
90 |     def gap_months(self) -> float | None:
91 |         """Months of runway left at the registered readout date. Negative = short.
92 | 
93 |         Computed against the CONSERVATIVE end of the burn band. Reporting the
94 |         optimistic end would flatter every row in the same direction.
95 |         """
96 |         end = self.runway.exhaustion(self.runway.months_low)
97 |         cat = self.catalyst_date
98 |         if end is None or cat is None:
99 |             return None
100 |         return (end - cat).days / (365.25 / 12)
101 | 
102 |     @property
103 |     def verdict(self) -> str:
104 |         g = self.gap_months
105 |         if g is None:
106 |             return "not computable"
107 |         if not self.runway.reliable:
108 |             return "not rankable (burn estimate unreliable)"
109 |         return "funded to catalyst" if g >= 0 else "financing required before catalyst"
110 | 
111 |     def lines(self) -> list[str]:
112 |         r, t = self.runway, self.trial
113 |         g = self.gap_months
114 |         out = [
115 |             f"{r.ticker}  {r.name}",
116 |             f"  liquidity      ${r.liquidity/1e6:,.0f}M  ({r.provenance['cash']} + {r.provenance['securities']}, as of {r.as_of})",
117 |             f"  burn band      ${r.burn_ttm_annual/1e6:,.0f}M/yr trailing, ${r.burn_recent_annual/1e6:,.0f}M/yr most recent quarter",
118 |             f"  runway         {r.months_low:.1f}-{r.months_high:.1f} months, exhausted {r.exhaustion(r.months_low)}",
119 |             f"  catalyst       {t['nct']}  {'/'.join(t['phases'])}  {t['status']}",
120 |             f"                 {t['title'][:70]}",
121 |             f"                 primary completion {t['pcd']} ({t['pcd_type'].lower()})",
122 |             f"  FUNDING GAP    {'n/a' if g is None else f'{g:+.1f} months'}   -> {self.verdict}",
123 |         ]
124 |         if self.history and self.history.revisions:
125 |             h = self.history
126 |             out += [
127 |                 f"  date integrity {len(h.revisions)} revisions across {h.n_versions} versions, "
128 |                 f"net slip {h.total_slip_days:+d} days, {h.n_late_moves} moved with under 90 days notice",
129 |             ]
130 |             if h.n_expired_carried:
131 |                 out.append(f"  *** EXPIRED     showed an already-passed completion date for up to "
132 |                            f"{h.max_days_expired} days before correcting it")
133 |             for rev in h.revisions[-4:]:
134 |                 if rev.carried_expired:
135 |                     notice = f"{rev.days_expired:>4}d EXPIRED"
136 |                 elif rev.held_days is None:
137 |                     notice = ""
138 |                 else:
139 |                     notice = f"{rev.held_days:>5}d notice"
140 |                 moved = "" if rev.moved_days is None else f"{rev.moved_days:+5d}d"
141 |                 out.append(f"                 v{rev.version:<4} {rev.submitted}  -> {rev.pcd}  {moved}  {notice}")
142 |         for n in r.notes:
143 |             out.append(f"  note           {n}")
144 |         return out
145 | 
146 | 
147 | def build(ticker: str, cik_map=None, with_history: bool = True) -> CatalystContract | None:
148 |     r = compute_runway(ticker, cik_map)
149 |     trials = find_trials(r.name)
150 |     if not trials:
151 |         return None
152 |     # The nearest live pivotal readout is the binding catalyst.
153 |     trial = trials[0]
154 |     hist = None
155 |     if with_history:
156 |         try:
157 |             hist = fetch_history(trial["nct"])
158 |         except (RuntimeError, ValueError):
159 |             pass  # history is an enrichment, not a precondition
160 |     return CatalystContract(runway=r, trial=trial, history=hist)
161 | 
162 | 
163 | def demo() -> None:
164 |     cik_map = ticker_to_cik()
165 |     built = 0
166 |     for ticker in ["SANA", "PRME", "RCKT", "NTLA"]:
167 |         c = build(ticker, cik_map)
168 |         if c is None:
169 |             print(f"{ticker}: no live pivotal trial matched by sponsor name\n")
170 |             continue
171 |         built += 1
172 |         assert c.runway.ticker == ticker
173 |         assert c.trial["nct"].startswith("NCT")
174 |         if c.gap_months is not None:
175 |             # Gap must equal exhaustion minus catalyst, to the day.
176 |             recomputed = (c.runway.exhaustion(c.runway.months_low) - c.catalyst_date).days
177 |             assert abs(recomputed / (365.25 / 12) - c.gap_months) < 1e-9
178 |         print("\n".join(c.lines()))
179 |         print()
180 |     assert built, "no contract assembled"
181 |     print("ok")
182 | 
183 | 
184 | if __name__ == "__main__":
185 |     demo()

1 | """Flatten a CatalystContract into the {metric_id: value} dict that scan_breaches
2 | consumes.
3 | 
4 | The contract already computes everything; this module is the translation layer that
5 | names each computed value with a stable metric_id so the ledger can address it.
6 | 
7 | Five metrics cover the funded-to-catalyst claim:
8 | 
9 |     gap_months           signed runway surplus at the registered readout date
10 |                          (negative means the money runs out first)
11 |     runway_months_low    conservative runway endpoint (higher-burn estimate)
12 |     burn_ttm_annual      trailing-twelve-month burn, annualised, in dollars
13 |     pcd_revisions        number of times the registered completion date moved
14 |     max_days_expired     longest stretch the registry showed a date that had passed
15 | 
16 | The first two come from Runway; the third from Runway as well; the last two come from
17 | TrialHistory. When history was not fetched (enrichment is optional), pcd_revisions and
18 | max_days_expired are absent from the packet rather than zero — zero would look like "no
19 | revisions ever recorded", which is a different claim.
20 | """
21 | from __future__ import annotations
22 | 
23 | from engine.gap import CatalystContract
24 | 
25 | 
26 | def to_packet(contract: CatalystContract) -> dict[str, float]:
27 |     """Return the flat {metric_id: value} dict for one CatalystContract.
28 | 
29 |     Only computable values are included. Callers that need to detect a missing
30 |     metric should check for key absence rather than a sentinel value.
31 |     """
32 |     r = contract.runway
33 |     packet: dict[str, float] = {}
34 | 
35 |     gap = contract.gap_months
36 |     if gap is not None:
37 |         packet["gap_months"] = gap
38 | 
39 |     if r.months_low is not None:
40 |         packet["runway_months_low"] = r.months_low
41 | 
42 |     # burn_ttm_annual is always present (compute_runway raises if no burn tag found).
43 |     packet["burn_ttm_annual"] = r.burn_ttm_annual
44 | 
45 |     h = contract.history
46 |     if h is not None:
47 |         packet["pcd_revisions"] = float(len(h.revisions))
48 |         packet["max_days_expired"] = float(h.max_days_expired)
49 | 
50 |     return packet
51 | 
52 | 
53 | def demo() -> None:
54 |     """Round-trip: build a contract, flatten it, confirm scan_breaches fires on a
55 |     deliberately out-of-range gap.
56 | 
57 |     The live network calls are the same ones gap.demo() already makes — the cache
58 |     covers them after the first run, so repeated runs are offline and instant.
59 |     """
60 |     from engine.gap import build
61 |     from engine.ledger import BeliefCard, BeliefLedger, scan_breaches
62 |     import tempfile, os
63 | 
64 |     # --- build a real contract ---
65 |     from engine.runway import ticker_to_cik
66 |     cik_map = ticker_to_cik()
67 |     contract = None
68 |     for ticker in ["RCKT", "NTLA", "SANA", "PRME"]:
69 |         contract = build(ticker, cik_map)
70 |         if contract is not None:
71 |             break
72 |     assert contract is not None, "no contract assembled"
73 | 
74 |     packet = to_packet(contract)
75 | 
76 |     # gap_months and runway_months_low must be present when computable.
77 |     assert "burn_ttm_annual" in packet, "burn always present"
78 |     if contract.gap_months is not None:
79 |         assert "gap_months" in packet
80 |         # Value must equal the contract's own property, to floating-point identity.
81 |         assert packet["gap_months"] == contract.gap_months
82 |     if contract.runway.months_low is not None:
83 |         assert "runway_months_low" in packet
84 |         assert packet["runway_months_low"] == contract.runway.months_low
85 |     if contract.history is not None:
86 |         assert "pcd_revisions" in packet
87 |         assert "max_days_expired" in packet
88 |         assert packet["pcd_revisions"] == len(contract.history.revisions)
89 | 
90 |     # All values must be plain floats (scan_breaches compares them with card thresholds).
91 |     for k, v in packet.items():
92 |         assert isinstance(v, float), f"{k}: expected float, got {type(v).__name__}"
93 | 
94 |     print(f"packet for {contract.runway.ticker}:")
95 |     for k, v in sorted(packet.items()):
96 |         print(f"  {k:<24} {v:>12.2f}")
97 | 
98 |     # --- breach round-trip ---
99 |     # Seed a card whose approved range sits ABOVE the actual gap_months value so
100 |     # that the packet triggers a breach deterministically, regardless of the live number.
101 |     gap = packet.get("gap_months", -1.0)
102 |     # Force an "under" breach: approve [gap + 5, gap + 20], then supply gap as the reading.
103 |     approved_low = gap + 5.0
104 |     approved_high = gap + 20.0
105 | 
106 |     card = BeliefCard(
107 |         card_id="test:funded_to_catalyst",
108 |         scope=f"company:{contract.runway.ticker}",
109 |         claim="This company reaches its primary readout before runway exhaustion.",
110 |         metric="gap_months",
111 |         expected_low=approved_low,
112 |         expected_high=approved_high,
113 |         driver="SEC XBRL liquidity vs ClinicalTrials.gov registered PCD",
114 |         confidence=4,
115 |         source="engine.contract.demo",
116 |         as_of=contract.runway.as_of,
117 |     )
118 | 
119 |     with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".jsonl", delete=False) as f:
120 |         ledger_path = f.name
121 |     try:
122 |         ledger = BeliefLedger(ledger_path)

126 |         breaches = scan_breaches(ledger.current(), packet)
127 |         assert len(breaches) == 1, f"expected exactly one breach, got {len(breaches)}"
128 |         b = breaches[0]

130 |         assert b.metric == "gap_months"
131 |         assert b.direction == "under"
132 |         assert b.observed == gap
133 |         assert b.expected_low == approved_low
134 |     finally:
135 |         os.unlink(ledger_path)
136 | 
137 |     print(f"\nbreach on gap_months = {gap:.2f} (approved floor {approved_low:.2f}): ok")
138 |     print("ok")

1 | """Cash runway from SEC XBRL company facts. Deterministic, from filed tags only.
2 | 
3 | No model computes any number here. Every figure traces to a us-gaap tag in a specific
4 | filing, and `Runway.provenance` names which tag each component came from, because the
5 | tags are not uniform across filers and a runway number whose numerator you cannot
6 | identify is not auditable.
7 | 
8 | The numerator is the hard part, and it is the opposite of what you would guess. Burn
9 | (`NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities`) is tagged by ~99% of clinical-stage
10 | filers. Liquid securities are not: there is no single tag, and roughly half of filers
11 | use none of them because they hold only cash. The ones that DO hold securities are the
12 | well-capitalized companies whose runway matters most, so the tag waterfall below is
13 | load-bearing rather than defensive. `CashCashEquivalentsAndShortTermInvestments` looks
14 | like the right tag and is never filed -- do not reach for it.
15 | 
16 | Burn is reported as a BAND, not a point. A company that just dosed first patient in a
17 | pivotal trial will burn well above its trailing rate; one that just booked a partnership
18 | upfront shows a positive operating quarter. A single point estimate implies a precision
19 | the filings do not support, and a ranked screen whose order flips under a 25% burn
20 | perturbation is not a screen. Rank on the interval.
21 | """
22 | from __future__ import annotations
23 | 
24 | import json
25 | import os
26 | import subprocess
27 | import time
28 | from dataclasses import dataclass, field
29 | from datetime import date, datetime
30 | 
31 | SEC = "https://data.sec.gov"
32 | # SEC's access policy requires a real contact string in the User-Agent.
33 | UA = os.environ.get("SEC_UA", "catalyst-integrity-desk kris10harim@gmail.com")
34 | CACHE = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(__file__)), "data", "cache")
35 | SLEEP = 0.12  # SEC allows 10 req/s
36 | 
37 | # Tried in order; first hit wins, and the winner is recorded in provenance.
38 | CASH_TAGS = [
39 |     "CashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue",
40 |     "CashCashEquivalentsRestrictedCashAndRestrictedCashEquivalents",
41 | ]
42 | # Summed, not first-wins: a filer can report several distinct securities buckets.
43 | SECURITIES_TAGS = [
44 |     "ShortTermInvestments",
45 |     "MarketableSecuritiesCurrent",
46 |     "AvailableForSaleSecuritiesDebtSecuritiesCurrent",
47 | ]
48 | # Counted toward runway only when opted in. Companies include >12mo maturities in
49 | # their own runway guidance, but calling it "liquidity" without saying so is a stretch.
50 | LONG_TERM_TAGS = ["LongTermInvestments", "MarketableSecuritiesNoncurrent"]
51 | 
52 | BURN_TAG = "NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities"
53 | # Fallback for the ~1% that omit the cash-flow tag: opex is an upper bound on cash burn
54 | # (it includes non-cash stock comp), so a runway built on it is conservative.
55 | BURN_FALLBACK_TAGS = ["OperatingExpenses", "ResearchAndDevelopmentExpense"]
56 | 
57 | DAYS_PER_MONTH = 365.25 / 12
58 | 
59 | 
60 | def _curl_json(url: str, retries: int = 3) -> dict:
61 |     """SEC's edge blocks some non-browser clients; curl with a contact UA is accepted.
62 |     Same shell-out rationale as the registry crawler."""
63 |     for attempt in range(retries):
64 |         p = subprocess.run(
65 |             ["curl", "-sS", "--fail", "--compressed", "--max-time", "60",
66 |              "-H", f"User-Agent: {UA}", url],
67 |             capture_output=True, text=True,
68 |         )
69 |         if p.returncode == 0:
70 |             try:
71 |                 return json.loads(p.stdout)
72 |             except json.JSONDecodeError:
73 |                 pass
74 |         if attempt == retries - 1:
75 |             raise RuntimeError(f"GET {url} failed: {p.stderr.strip() or p.returncode}")
76 |         time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
77 |     raise RuntimeError("unreachable")
78 | 
79 | 
80 | def _cached(name: str, url: str) -> dict:
81 |     os.makedirs(CACHE, exist_ok=True)
82 |     path = os.path.join(CACHE, name)
83 |     if os.path.exists(path):
84 |         with open(path) as f:
85 |             return json.load(f)
86 |     time.sleep(SLEEP)
87 |     d = _curl_json(url)
88 |     with open(path, "w") as f:
89 |         json.dump(d, f)
90 |     return d

93 | def ticker_to_cik() -> dict[str, str]:
94 |     """SEC's own ticker map. CIKs are zero-padded to 10 digits in the API paths."""
95 |     d = _cached("company_tickers.json", "https://www.sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json")
96 |     return {v["ticker"].upper(): f"{int(v['cik_str']):010d}" for v in d.values()}
97 | 
98 | 
99 | def company_facts(cik: str) -> dict:
100 |     return _cached(f"facts-{cik}.json", f"{SEC}/api/xbrl/companyfacts/CIK{cik}.json")
101 | 
102 | 
103 | def _d(s: str) -> date:
104 |     return datetime.strptime(s, "%Y-%m-%d").date()
105 | 
106 | 
107 | def _usd(facts: dict, tag: str) -> list[dict]:
108 |     return facts.get("facts", {}).get("us-gaap", {}).get(tag, {}).get("units", {}).get("USD", [])
109 | 
110 | 
111 | def _latest_instant(facts: dict, tag: str) -> tuple[float, str] | None:
112 |     """Most recent point-in-time value (balance-sheet items have no `start`)."""
113 |     pts = [f for f in _usd(facts, tag) if "start" not in f and f.get("end")]
114 |     if not pts:
115 |         return None
116 |     best = max(pts, key=lambda f: (f["end"], f.get("fy") or 0))
117 |     return float(best["val"]), best["end"]
118 | 
119 | 
120 | def _quarterly_flows(facts: dict, tag: str) -> list[dict]:
121 |     """True quarterly values, newest first, recovered by differencing the YTD series.
122 | 
123 |     Cash-flow statements are cumulative within a fiscal year. A 10-Q reports
124 |     year-to-date, so the only fact that is natively ~90 days long is Q1; Q2 arrives as
125 |     a 6-month figure, Q3 as 9-month, Q4 inside the 10-K as 12-month. Filtering on a
126 |     60-120 day span therefore does NOT return the last four quarters -- it returns Q1
127 |     of four consecutive years, and summing those produces a plausible-looking annual
128 |     burn that is not the trailing twelve months of anything.
129 | 
130 |     Facts within one fiscal year share a `start`, so grouping on `start` recovers the
131 |     cumulative progression, and consecutive differences recover the quarters:
132 | 
133 |         Q1 = YTD(3mo)                Q3 = YTD(9mo) - YTD(6mo)
134 |         Q2 = YTD(6mo) - YTD(3mo)     Q4 = FY(12mo) - YTD(9mo)
135 |     """
136 |     by_start: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = {}
137 |     for f in _usd(facts, tag):
138 |         if not (f.get("start") and f.get("end")):
139 |             continue
140 |         # Later filings restate; keep the most recently reported value for each period.
141 |         by_start.setdefault(f["start"], {})[f["end"]] = float(f["val"])
142 | 
143 |     quarters: list[dict] = []
144 |     for start, ends in by_start.items():
145 |         prev_end, prev_val = start, 0.0
146 |         for end in sorted(ends):
147 |             span = (_d(end) - _d(prev_end)).days
148 |             if 60 <= span <= 120:      # one quarter's worth of elapsed time
149 |                 quarters.append({"start": prev_end, "end": end,
150 |                                  "val": ends[end] - prev_val})
151 |             prev_end, prev_val = end, ends[end]
152 | 
153 |     seen, uniq = set(), []
154 |     for q in sorted(quarters, key=lambda q: q["end"], reverse=True):
155 |         if q["end"] not in seen:
156 |             seen.add(q["end"])
157 |             uniq.append(q)
158 |     return uniq
159 | 
160 | 
161 | @dataclass
162 | class Runway:
163 |     ticker: str
164 |     cik: str
165 |     name: str
166 |     as_of: str
167 |     cash: float
168 |     securities: float
169 |     burn_ttm_annual: float        # trailing four quarters, annualized
170 |     burn_recent_annual: float     # most recent quarter, annualized
171 |     provenance: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
172 |     notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
173 |     inflow_quarters: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
174 | 
175 |     @property
176 |     def liquidity(self) -> float:
177 |         return self.cash + self.securities
178 | 
179 |     def _months(self, annual_burn: float) -> float | None:
180 |         if annual_burn <= 0:
181 |             return None  # cash-flow positive over this window; runway is not the binding question
182 |         return self.liquidity / (annual_burn / 12)
183 | 
184 |     @property
185 |     def months_low(self) -> float | None:
186 |         """Shorter runway of the two burn estimates -- the conservative end."""
187 |         got = [m for m in (self._months(self.burn_ttm_annual),
188 |                            self._months(self.burn_recent_annual)) if m is not None]
189 |         return min(got) if got else None
190 | 
191 |     @property
192 |     def months_high(self) -> float | None:
193 |         got = [m for m in (self._months(self.burn_ttm_annual),
194 |                            self._months(self.burn_recent_annual)) if m is not None]
195 |         return max(got) if got else None
196 | 
197 |     @property
198 |     def burn_unstable(self) -> bool:
199 |         """True when the two burn estimates disagree by more than 3x.
200 | 
201 |         This is the honest answer to "your burn number is wrong for exactly the
202 |         companies that matter". A collaboration upfront, a milestone, or a Phase 3
203 |         ramp makes one quarter unrepresentative, and the disagreement between the
204 |         trailing-year and most-recent-quarter estimates is the cheapest available
205 |         detector. A row flagged here must not be ranked on its point estimate.
206 |         """
207 |         lo, hi = self.months_low, self.months_high
208 |         return lo is not None and hi is not None and lo > 0 and hi / lo > 3
209 | 
210 |     @property
211 |     def reliable(self) -> bool:
212 |         """Whether this row may be RANKED, as opposed to merely reported.
213 | 
214 |         Cash divided by burn is only a runway when the denominator is an operating
215 |         burn. A partnership upfront or milestone inside the trailing year makes it
216 |         something else: Arrowhead's 2025 Sarepta payment nets its trailing burn to
217 |         near zero and produces a 1,100-month runway, which is arithmetically correct
218 |         and financially meaningless. Those rows stay visible with the reason attached,
219 |         because a screen that silently drops its hard cases is worse than one that
220 |         shows them -- but they never carry a rank.
221 |         """
222 |         return (self.months_low is not None
223 |                 and not self.inflow_quarters
224 |                 and not self.burn_unstable)
225 | 
226 |     def exhaustion(self, months: float | None) -> date | None:
227 |         if months is None:
228 |             return None
229 |         return _d(self.as_of) + __import__("datetime").timedelta(days=months * DAYS_PER_MONTH)
230 | 
231 |     def __str__(self) -> str:
232 |         if self.months_low is None:
233 |             return f"{self.ticker:6} {self.name[:28]:28} cash-flow positive over both windows"
234 |         flag = "  UNSTABLE BURN" if self.burn_unstable else ""
235 |         return (f"{self.ticker:6} {self.name[:28]:28} "
236 |                 f"${self.liquidity/1e6:8,.0f}M  "
237 |                 f"runway {self.months_low:5.1f}-{self.months_high:5.1f} mo  "
238 |                 f"(to {self.exhaustion(self.months_low)}){flag}")
239 | 
240 | 
241 | def compute_runway(ticker: str, cik_map: dict[str, str] | None = None,
242 |                    include_long_term: bool = False) -> Runway:
243 |     cik_map = cik_map or ticker_to_cik()
244 |     ticker = ticker.upper()
245 |     if ticker not in cik_map:
246 |         raise KeyError(f"{ticker} not in SEC ticker map")
247 |     cik = cik_map[ticker]
248 |     facts = company_facts(cik)
249 | 
250 |     prov, notes = {}, []
251 | 
252 |     # Pick the cash tag with the MOST RECENT balance date, not the first one that
253 |     # exists. Filers migrate between tags: Arrowhead stopped filing
254 |     # CashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue in 2024 and moved to the restricted-cash-
255 |     # inclusive tag, so a first-wins waterfall silently returned a two-year-old $69M
256 |     # balance instead of the current $1.8B, and dated the whole record to 2024. A
257 |     # stale numerator does not look wrong -- it looks like a company about to die,
258 |     # which is precisely the row a screen like this puts on screen first.
259 |     candidates = [(tag, hit) for tag in CASH_TAGS if (hit := _latest_instant(facts, tag))]
260 |     cash, as_of = 0.0, ""
261 |     if candidates:
262 |         tag, (cash, as_of) = max(candidates, key=lambda c: c[1][1])
263 |         prov["cash"] = tag
264 |         # This tag bundles restricted cash, which cannot fund operations. Net it out
265 |         # when the filer reports it separately on the same date.
266 |         if "RestrictedCash" in tag:
267 |             r = _latest_instant(facts, "RestrictedCashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue")
268 |             if r and r[1] == as_of:
269 |                 cash -= r[0]
270 |                 prov["cash"] += " less RestrictedCashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue"
271 |     else:
272 |         notes.append("no cash tag found")
273 | 
274 |     securities, sec_tags = 0.0, []
275 |     for tag in SECURITIES_TAGS + (LONG_TERM_TAGS if include_long_term else []):
276 |         hit = _latest_instant(facts, tag)
277 |         # Only count a securities balance struck on the same date as the cash balance,
278 |         # or the numerator mixes two different quarters.
279 |         if hit and hit[1] == as_of:
280 |             securities += hit[0]
281 |             sec_tags.append(tag)
282 |     prov["securities"] = "+".join(sec_tags) if sec_tags else "none"
283 |     if not sec_tags:
284 |         notes.append("no securities tag on the cash date (cash-only, or fragmented tagging)")
285 | 
286 |     flows = _quarterly_flows(facts, BURN_TAG)
287 |     prov["burn"] = BURN_TAG
288 |     if not flows:
289 |         for tag in BURN_FALLBACK_TAGS:
290 |             flows = [{**f, "val": -abs(float(f["val"]))} for f in _quarterly_flows(facts, tag)]
291 |             if flows:
292 |                 prov["burn"] = f"{tag} (fallback, upper bound on cash burn)"
293 |                 notes.append("cash-flow tag absent; burn approximated from expense tag")
294 |                 break
295 |     if not flows:
296 |         raise ValueError(f"{ticker}: no usable burn tag")
297 | 
298 |     ttm = flows[:4]
299 |     # Operating cash flow is negative for a company that burns. Flip the sign so a
300 |     # positive burn number means money going out, which is what the ratio expects.
301 |     burn_ttm_annual = -sum(float(f["val"]) for f in ttm) * (4 / len(ttm))
302 |     burn_recent_annual = -float(flows[0]["val"]) * 4
303 |     if len(ttm) < 4:
304 |         notes.append(f"only {len(ttm)} quarters available; TTM scaled up")
305 |     inflow_quarters = [f["end"] for f in ttm if float(f["val"]) > 0]
306 |     if inflow_quarters:
307 |         # A cash-positive quarter inside the trailing year is almost always a
308 |         # partnership upfront or milestone, not a change in operating economics.
309 |         # It deflates the burn estimate and inflates runway; say so on the row.
310 |         notes.append(f"cash-positive operating quarter(s) in TTM window: "
311 |                      f"{', '.join(inflow_quarters)} - likely a one-time inflow")
312 | 
313 |     return Runway(
314 |         ticker=ticker, cik=cik, name=facts.get("entityName", ""),
315 |         as_of=as_of or flows[0]["end"], cash=cash, securities=securities,
316 |         burn_ttm_annual=burn_ttm_annual, burn_recent_annual=burn_recent_annual,
317 |         provenance=prov, notes=notes, inflow_quarters=inflow_quarters,
318 |     )
319 | 
320 | 
321 | def demo() -> None:
322 |     """Self-check on real filers. Values are not hardcoded -- the assertions check
323 |     internal consistency and sane magnitudes, so this keeps passing as filings roll."""
324 |     cik_map = ticker_to_cik()
325 |     assert len(cik_map) > 5000, len(cik_map)
326 |     assert "MRNA" in cik_map
327 | 
328 |     # company_tickers.json lags: it is missing live filers (Amicus/FOLD is absent from
329 |     # all 10,426 entries). Fine for a demo list, wrong as a universe source -- the
330 |     # universe comes from DERA sub.txt by SIC code, keyed on CIK, with no ticker.
331 |     # Clinical-stage and pre-revenue on purpose. Commercial names (MRNA, SRPT, IONS,
332 |     # ALNY, ARWR) get flagged constantly because product revenue and partnership
333 |     # upfronts land in operating cash flow, which is correct behaviour and also the
334 |     # wrong population: for a company with no product, operating cash flow IS burn.
335 |     tickers = ["BEAM", "NTLA", "SANA", "RCKT", "DYN", "KYMR", "NUVL", "PRME",
336 |                "ARVN", "EDIT", "CRSP", "VOR"]
337 |     rows = []
338 |     for t in tickers:
339 |         try:
340 |             rows.append(compute_runway(t, cik_map))
341 |         except (KeyError, ValueError) as e:
342 |             print(f"  {t}: skipped ({e})")
343 | 
344 |     assert rows, "no company resolved"
345 |     for r in rows:
346 |         assert r.cash >= 0 and r.securities >= 0
347 |         assert r.liquidity >= r.cash
348 |         if r.months_low is not None:
349 |             assert r.months_low <= r.months_high
350 |         assert r.provenance.get("cash"), r.ticker
351 |     # Only rankable rows must be sane. Unreliable ones are allowed to be absurd --
352 |     # that is what makes them unreliable, and the flag is the point.
353 |     for r in [r for r in rows if r.reliable]:
354 |         assert 0 < r.months_low < 600, (r.ticker, r.months_low)
355 | 
356 |     rankable = [r for r in rows if r.reliable]
357 |     flagged = [r for r in rows if not r.reliable]
358 |     assert rankable, "every row was flagged; the guards are too aggressive"
359 | 
360 |     print(f"RANKED  ({len(rankable)} of {len(rows)} rows carry a rank)")
361 |     for r in sorted(rankable, key=lambda r: r.months_low):
362 |         print(f"  {r}")
363 |     if flagged:
364 |         print(f"\nREPORTED, NOT RANKED  ({len(flagged)})")
365 |         for r in flagged:
366 |             print(f"  {r}")
367 |             for n in r.notes:
368 |                 print(f"         {n}")
369 |     print()
370 |     for r in rows:
371 |         print(f"  {r.ticker:6} as_of {r.as_of}  cash={r.provenance['cash']}  "
372 |               f"sec={r.provenance['securities']}")
373 |         for n in r.notes:
374 |             print(f"         note: {n}")

1 | """Append-only, hash-chained BeliefCard ledger — the governed record of what
2 | the engine *believes* about a catalyst contract.
3 | 
4 | A BeliefCard is one claim: "RCKT is funded to its primary completion date with
5 | a gap of at least zero months." The engine measures the metric; the card holds
6 | the *expected range plus the human rationale*. The Challenge step (later,
7 | Granite) reads that rationale to judge whether a breach actually matters — the
8 | card is what keeps the AI's job semantic, not a threshold check.
9 | 
10 | The ledger is event-sourced: every CREATE / UPDATE / RETIRE is appended, never
11 | mutated, and hash-chained so tampering is detectable. Only an explicit human
12 | approval appends an UPDATE — that is the whole governance guarantee.
13 | 
14 | Store: one JSONL file, one event per line. Human-readable, `cat`-able in a demo,
15 | dependency-free (stdlib). ponytail: swap to sqlite3 only if querying history at
16 | scale becomes the bottleneck — the append/verify logic stays identical.
17 | """
18 | from __future__ import annotations
19 | 
20 | import hashlib
21 | import json
22 | import os
23 | import time
24 | from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict, replace
25 | 
26 | GENESIS_HASH = "0" * 64
27 | CREATE, UPDATE, RETIRE = "CREATE", "UPDATE", "RETIRE"
28 | 
29 | 
30 | @dataclass
31 | class BeliefCard:
32 |     card_id: str
33 |     scope: str            # "company:RCKT" or "trial:NCT04248439"
34 |     claim: str            # the belief in words — the rationale Granite must read
35 |     metric: str           # engine metric_id, e.g. "gap_months"
36 |     expected_low: float
37 |     expected_high: float
38 |     driver: str
39 |     confidence: int       # 1 (weak) .. 5 (high)
40 |     source: str
41 |     as_of: str
42 |     status: str = "active"   # active | under_review | retired
43 |     version: int = 1
44 | 
45 |     def in_range(self, value: float) -> bool:
46 |         return self.expected_low <= value <= self.expected_high
47 | 
48 | 
49 | @dataclass
50 | class Breach:
51 |     card_id: str
52 |     metric: str
53 |     observed: float
54 |     expected_low: float
55 |     expected_high: float
56 |     direction: str        # "over" | "under"
57 | 
58 | 
59 | def _canonical(payload: dict) -> str:
60 |     return json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
61 | 
62 | 
63 | def _hash(prev_hash: str, payload: dict) -> str:
64 |     return hashlib.sha256((prev_hash + _canonical(payload)).encode()).hexdigest()
65 | 
66 | 
67 | class BeliefLedger:
68 |     def __init__(self, path: str):
69 |         self.path = path
70 | 
71 |     # --- low-level read ---
72 |     def _entries(self) -> list[dict]:
73 |         if not os.path.exists(self.path):
74 |             return []
75 |         with open(self.path) as f:
76 |             return [json.loads(line) for line in f if line.strip()]
77 | 
78 |     def _last(self) -> dict | None:
79 |         entries = self._entries()
80 |         return entries[-1] if entries else None
81 | 
82 |     # --- append (the only writer) ---
83 |     def _append(self, event: str, card: BeliefCard, author: str,
84 |                 triggered_by: str | None, reason: str | None, ts: float | None) -> dict:
85 |         last = self._last()
86 |         prev_hash = last["entry_hash"] if last else GENESIS_HASH
87 |         seq = (last["seq"] + 1) if last else 0
88 |         payload = {
89 |             "seq": seq,
90 |             "ts": ts if ts is not None else time.time(),
91 |             "event": event,
92 |             "author": author,
93 |             "triggered_by": triggered_by,
94 |             "reason": reason,
95 |             "card": asdict(card),
96 |             "prev_hash": prev_hash,
97 |         }
98 |         entry = {**payload, "entry_hash": _hash(prev_hash, payload)}
99 |         os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(self.path) or ".", exist_ok=True)
100 |         with open(self.path, "a") as f:
101 |             f.write(json.dumps(entry) + "\n")
102 |         return entry
103 | 
104 |     def create(self, card: BeliefCard, author: str = "seed", ts: float | None = None) -> dict:
105 |         if card.card_id in self.current():
106 |             raise ValueError(f"card {card.card_id} already exists")
107 |         return self._append(CREATE, replace(card, version=1, status="active"),
108 |                             author, None, None, ts)
109 | 
110 |     def update(self, card: BeliefCard, author: str, triggered_by: str | None,
111 |                reason: str, ts: float | None = None) -> dict:
112 |         """Append an approved change. Bumps version off the current card.
113 |         `author` should be a human (e.g. "human:kristen") — this is the gate."""
114 |         cur = self.current().get(card.card_id)
115 |         if cur is None:
116 |             raise ValueError(f"card {card.card_id} does not exist")
117 |         bumped = replace(card, version=cur.version + 1)
118 |         return self._append(UPDATE, bumped, author, triggered_by, reason, ts)
119 | 
120 |     def retire(self, card_id: str, author: str, reason: str, ts: float | None = None) -> dict:
121 |         cur = self.current().get(card_id)
122 |         if cur is None:
123 |             raise ValueError(f"card {card_id} does not exist")
124 |         return self._append(RETIRE, replace(cur, status="retired", version=cur.version + 1),
125 |                             author, None, reason, ts)
126 | 
127 |     # --- read models (fold events) ---
128 |     def current(self) -> dict[str, BeliefCard]:
129 |         """Latest card state per id, excluding retired ones."""
130 |         state: dict[str, BeliefCard] = {}
131 |         for e in self._entries():
132 |             card = BeliefCard(**e["card"])
133 |             if card.status == "retired":
134 |                 state.pop(card.card_id, None)
135 |             else:
136 |                 state[card.card_id] = card
137 |         return state
138 | 
139 |     def history(self, card_id: str) -> list[dict]:
140 |         return [e for e in self._entries() if e["card"]["card_id"] == card_id]
141 | 
142 |     # --- integrity ---
143 |     def verify(self) -> bool:
144 |         """Recompute the whole chain; True iff untampered. Never raises.
145 | 
146 |         Editing a byte of the file usually breaks the JSON or drops a key rather
147 |         than producing a well-formed entry with a wrong hash, so an integrity
148 |         check that only handles the tidy case raises on the most likely kind of
149 |         tampering. A ledger that cannot be parsed is a ledger that cannot be
150 |         verified, and the honest answer to "is this intact" is no, not a
151 |         traceback. Callers render this as a status; a status that throws is worse
152 |         than one that reports false.
153 |         """
154 |         try:
155 |             entries = self._entries()
156 |         except Exception:                                       # noqa: BLE001
157 |             return False
158 | 
159 |         prev_hash = GENESIS_HASH
160 |         for i, e in enumerate(entries):
161 |             try:
162 |                 if e["seq"] != i or e["prev_hash"] != prev_hash:
163 |                     return False
164 |                 payload = {k: e[k] for k in
165 |                            ("seq", "ts", "event", "author", "triggered_by", "reason", "card", "prev_hash")}
166 |                 if _hash(prev_hash, payload) != e["entry_hash"]:
167 |                     return False
168 |                 prev_hash = e["entry_hash"]
169 |             except Exception:                                   # noqa: BLE001
170 |                 return False
171 |         return True
172 | 
173 | 
174 | def scan_breaches(cards: dict[str, BeliefCard], packet_flat: dict[str, float]) -> list[Breach]:
175 |     """Deterministic breach trigger: which active cards have their metric outside
176 |     the expected range *right now*. This only fires the Challenge — whether the
177 |     breach MATTERS is Granite's judgment call against the card's rationale."""
178 |     breaches = []
179 |     for card in cards.values():
180 |         if card.status != "active":
181 |             continue
182 |         v = packet_flat.get(card.metric)
183 |         if v is None:
184 |             continue
185 |         if not card.in_range(v):
186 |             breaches.append(Breach(
187 |                 card_id=card.card_id, metric=card.metric, observed=float(v),
188 |                 expected_low=card.expected_low, expected_high=card.expected_high,
189 |                 direction="over" if v > card.expected_high else "under",
190 |             ))
191 |     return breaches
192 |

Contents of file orchestrator/redline.py:

1 | """Redline: detect when a recomputed CatalystContract breaches its approved belief,
2 | then build a ChallengeCard for human review.
3 | 
4 | Entry point: `run_redline(approved_contract, new_contract, card, classifier)`.
5 | 
6 | The engine produces two metric packets -- one for the previously approved state of the
7 | contract, one for the recomputed state after an amendment or new filing. `as_directions`
8 | turns the per-metric change into a description suitable for Granite: the direction and
9 | size of the move in words, never a measured value. The application renders every figure.
10 | 
11 | Metric definitions (required because the signs are non-obvious):
12 |     gap_months           signed months of runway surplus at the registered primary
13 |                          completion date; negative means cash is exhausted BEFORE the
14 |                          readout, not that the trial is running late.
15 |     runway_months_low    months until cash exhaustion at the conservative (high-burn)
16 |                          end of the burn band.
17 |     burn_ttm_annual      trailing-twelve-month annualised operating cash outflow, in
18 |                          dollars; higher is worse.
19 |     pcd_revisions        cumulative count of times the registry date has moved.
20 |     max_days_expired     longest continuous stretch, in days, that the registry showed
21 |                          a primary completion date that had already passed.
22 | """
23 | from __future__ import annotations
24 | 
25 | from dataclasses import dataclass
26 | 
27 | from engine.contract import to_packet
28 | from engine.gap import CatalystContract
29 | from engine.ledger import BeliefCard, Breach
30 | from orchestrator.challenge import ChallengeCard, build_challenge
31 | from orchestrator.classifier import StubClassifier
32 | 
33 | # Metric definitions surfaced in the brief so the model knows what the sign means.
34 | # Indexed by metric_id; each value is the one-line definition.
35 | _METRIC_DEFS: dict[str, str] = {
36 |     "gap_months": (
37 |         "signed months of runway surplus at the registered primary completion date; "
38 |         "negative means cash is exhausted BEFORE the readout, not that the trial is "
39 |         "running late"
40 |     ),
41 |     "runway_months_low": (
42 |         "months until cash exhaustion at the conservative end of the burn band; "
43 |         "lower is worse"
44 |     ),
45 |     "burn_ttm_annual": (
46 |         "trailing-twelve-month annualised operating cash outflow in dollars; "
47 |         "higher means the company is spending more"
48 |     ),
49 |     "pcd_revisions": (
50 |         "cumulative number of times the sponsor has revised the registered primary "
51 |         "completion date; a rising count means the sponsor is revising more"
52 |     ),
53 |     "max_days_expired": (
54 |         "longest continuous stretch in days that the registry showed a completion date "
55 |         "that had already passed; higher means the sponsor carried a stale date longer"
56 |     ),
57 | }
58 | 
59 | 
60 | def _direction(pct_change: float) -> str:
61 |     """Direction and magnitude of a metric shift, in words.
62 | 
63 |     Buckets are deliberately coarse -- the model reasons over these, so they must
64 |     not be reconstructible into a figure. The same vocabulary as scenario.py's
65 |     _direction, adapted for metrics that move in both directions.
66 |     """
67 |     if pct_change != pct_change:    # nan
68 |         return "not comparable"
69 |     verb = "rises" if pct_change > 0 else "falls"
70 |     mag = abs(pct_change)
71 |     if mag < 1:
72 |         return "essentially unchanged"
73 |     if mag < 5:
74 |         return f"{verb} slightly"
75 |     if mag < 15:
76 |         return f"{verb} moderately"
77 |     if mag < 30:
78 |         return f"{verb} materially"
79 |     return f"{verb} sharply"
80 | 
81 | 
82 | def _pct_change(before: float, after: float) -> float:
83 |     """Signed percentage change. Returns nan when the denominator is zero."""
84 |     if before == 0.0:
85 |         return float("nan")
86 |     return (after / before - 1.0) * 100
87 | 
88 | 
89 | def as_directions(before: dict[str, float], after: dict[str, float]) -> str:
90 |     """Express each metric change as a direction label, never a value.
91 | 
92 |     Only metrics present in both packets are reported. Each line names the metric,
93 |     its one-line definition (so the model knows what the sign means), and the
94 |     direction of the move.
95 | 
96 |     The application renders every number. This string contains none.
97 |     """
98 |     lines = []
99 |     for metric in sorted(set(before) & set(after)):
100 |         b, a = before[metric], after[metric]
101 |         direction = _direction(_pct_change(b, a))
102 |         defn = _METRIC_DEFS.get(metric, "")
103 |         if defn:
104 |             lines.append(f"  {metric} ({defn}): {direction}")
105 |         else:
106 |             lines.append(f"  {metric}: {direction}")
107 |     return "\n".join(lines) if lines else "  no shared metrics changed"
108 | 
109 | 
110 | @dataclass
111 | class ContractDelta:
112 |     """Before and after packets for one amendment event."""
113 |     approved: CatalystContract    # the contract as it was when last approved
114 |     recomputed: CatalystContract  # the contract after the new filing or amendment
115 | 
116 |     @property
117 |     def before(self) -> dict[str, float]:
118 |         return to_packet(self.approved)
119 | 
120 |     @property
121 |     def after(self) -> dict[str, float]:
122 |         return to_packet(self.recomputed)
123 | 
124 |     def directions(self) -> str:
125 |         """What moved, in words. Fed to Granite; never contains a measured value."""
126 |         return as_directions(self.before, self.after)
127 | 
128 | 
129 | def _breach_for_gap(card: BeliefCard, new_packet: dict[str, float]) -> Breach | None:
130 |     """Return the gap_months breach if one exists, else None.
131 | 
132 |     The funding gap is the primary signal. When it is absent (not computable) the
133 |     breach cannot be constructed and the caller falls back to other metrics.
134 |     """
135 |     gap = new_packet.get("gap_months")
136 |     if gap is None:
137 |         return None
138 |     if card.in_range(gap):
139 |         return None
140 |     return Breach(
141 |         card_id=card.card_id,
142 |         metric="gap_months",
143 |         observed=gap,
144 |         expected_low=card.expected_low,
145 |         expected_high=card.expected_high,
146 |         direction="over" if gap > card.expected_high else "under",
147 |     )
148 | 
149 | 
150 | def run_redline(
151 |     delta: ContractDelta,
152 |     card: BeliefCard,
153 |     classifier=None,
154 |     context: dict | None = None,
155 | ) -> ChallengeCard | None:
156 |     """Build a ChallengeCard when a recomputed contract breaches the approved belief.
157 | 
158 |     Returns None when no breach is detected -- the contract still satisfies the
159 |     approved range and no review is needed.
160 | 
161 |     `context` is forwarded to Granite as supplementary evidence (e.g. news snippets).
162 |     The directions string is merged into it so the prompt carries both the standing
163 |     belief and a description of what moved.
164 | 
165 |     The classifier defaults to StubClassifier so the loop is demoable without
166 |     credentials; swap in a GraniteClassifier for live judgment.
167 |     """
168 |     classifier = classifier or StubClassifier()
169 | 
170 |     breach = _breach_for_gap(card, delta.after)
171 |     if breach is None:
172 |         return None
173 | 
174 |     # Merge the directions summary into context so the Granite user prompt carries
175 |     # the description of what moved alongside the standing belief and breach reading.
176 |     ctx = dict(context or {})
177 |     ctx["directions"] = delta.directions()
178 | 
179 |     return build_challenge(card, breach, ctx, classifier)

1 | """IBM Granite (watsonx.ai) plugged into the classifier seam.
2 | 
3 | Same `.classify(card, breach, context)` signature as StubClassifier, so swapping
4 | it in is a one-line change at the call site and the pipeline, ledger, and UI are
5 | untouched.
6 | 
7 | What Granite is actually for here: the stub compares numbers, which is a Python
8 | `if`. Granite's job is the semantic step the stub cannot do — read the card's
9 | claim and driver and judge whether the breach changes the *story*. A contract can
10 | sit outside its numeric band and still be fine if the thesis said so, and a small
11 | drift can be fatal if it contradicts the stated rationale. That judgment is the
12 | whole reason a model is in this loop.
13 | 
14 | Provenance rule: Granite emits NO numbers. Every figure in the final artifact is
15 | rendered by application code from the engine's own output. A rationale containing
16 | a digit is rejected as fabrication risk and the run falls back to the stub. See
17 | `_NUMBER` below.
18 | 
19 | Credentials (never hardcode):
20 |     export WATSONX_API_KEY=...
21 |     export WATSONX_PROJECT_ID=...
22 |     export WATSONX_URL=https://us-south.ml.cloud.ibm.com   # your region
23 | """
24 | from __future__ import annotations
25 | 
26 | import json
27 | import os
28 | import re
29 | import sys
30 | import time
31 | import urllib.parse
32 | import urllib.request
33 | 
34 | from engine.ledger import BeliefCard, Breach
35 | from orchestrator.classifier import (
36 |     LABELS, Classification, StubClassifier,
37 |     DIRECT_CONTRADICTION, ASSUMPTION_WEAKENED, ASSUMPTION_STRENGTHENED, NEW_MATERIAL_EVIDENCE,
38 | )
39 | 
40 | IAM_URL = "https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token"
41 | API_VERSION = "2024-10-08"
42 | # granite-3-8b-instruct still answers but watsonx returns a withdrawn-state warning for
43 | # it, and a withdrawn model can be pulled. Not something to discover while recording the
44 | # submission demo.
45 | DEFAULT_MODEL = "ibm/granite-4-h-small"
46 | 
47 | # A number is fabricated if it did not come from the input. Quoting a figure that the
48 | # card's own claim already contains ("acceptable up to ~32%") is not invention, it is
49 | # quotation, and rejecting it throws away good judgment. What must never survive is a
50 | # figure the model produced on its own.
51 | # Compared as whole numbers, not as substrings. Substring containment let real
52 | # fabrications through: a model writing "5%" passed because the input mentioned
53 | # var_95_1d_dollar, and "3%" passed because it mentioned a 30-day window. Both
54 | # were invented trade sizes presented as if measured.
55 | _NUMBER_RUN = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
56 | 
57 | 
58 | def _numbers_in(text: str) -> set[str]:
59 |     return set(_NUMBER_RUN.findall(text))
60 | 
61 | # "1." at the start of a line is an option enumerator, not a claim about the book.
62 | # Stripped before the number guard runs so numbered options do not read as fabrication.
63 | _LIST_MARKER = re.compile(r"^\s*\d+[.)]\s+", re.M)
64 | 
65 | 
66 | def _fabricated(rationale: str, card: BeliefCard, breach: Breach) -> list[str]:
67 |     """Numbers in the rationale that appear nowhere in what the model was given."""
68 |     source = " ".join(str(x) for x in (
69 |         card.claim, card.driver, card.metric, card.confidence,
70 |         breach.observed, breach.expected_low, breach.expected_high,
71 |         # the model sees these rounded in the prompt, so allow both renderings
72 |         round(breach.observed), round(breach.expected_low), round(breach.expected_high),
73 |     ))
74 |     allowed = _numbers_in(source)
75 |     return [n for n in _NUMBER_RUN.findall(rationale) if n not in allowed]

77 | SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
78 | You are the challenge partner on a catalyst integrity desk. A deterministic engine has \
79 | already recomputed a catalyst contract and detected that something has left its approved \
80 | range. You do not compute, restate, or estimate any number.
81 | 
82 | Your job is the judgment the arithmetic cannot make: read the standing belief's claim \
83 | and its driver, then decide what the drift does to that belief's story.
84 | 
85 | METRIC DEFINITIONS (so you know what the sign means before you classify):
86 | - gap_months: signed months of runway surplus at the registered primary completion date. \
87 | Negative means cash is exhausted BEFORE the readout. It does not mean the trial is \
88 | running late. A negative gap is a funding shortfall, not a clinical delay.
89 | - runway_months_low: months until cash exhaustion at the conservative (high-burn) end of \
90 | the burn band. Lower is worse.
91 | - burn_ttm_annual: trailing-twelve-month annualised operating cash outflow. Higher means \
92 | the company is spending more.
93 | - pcd_revisions: cumulative count of times the sponsor revised the registered completion \
94 | date. Rising means more revisions.
95 | - max_days_expired: longest continuous stretch in days that the registry showed a \
96 | completion date that had already passed. Higher means the sponsor carried a stale date \
97 | longer.
98 | 
99 | Choose exactly one label:
100 | - direct_contradiction: the reading is incompatible with the claim as written.
101 | - assumption_weakened: the claim can still hold, but the reasoning behind it is eroding.
102 | - assumption_strengthened: the drift is in the direction the claim predicted.
103 | - new_material_evidence: the drift raises something the claim never accounted for.
104 | 
105 | Rules:
106 | - Reason from the claim's wording and its driver, not from how far the number moved. \
107 | A contract outside its band may still be acceptable if the claim anticipated the \
108 | condition; a small move can be serious if it undercuts the stated reason for the belief.
109 | - Weigh conviction: a low-conviction driver supporting a strong conclusion is a \
110 | weaker story than a high-conviction one.
111 | 
112 | HARD CONSTRAINT ON THE RATIONALE — this one is not stylistic, it is a correctness rule:
113 | Your rationale must contain NO digits. Not one. Do not restate the reading, do not quote \
114 | the band, do not echo a figure that appears in the claim itself. The desk's engine owns \
115 | every number and renders them beside your text; a number written by you is treated as \
116 | fabricated and your entire answer is discarded.
117 | 
118 | Say it in words instead:
119 | - NOT "gap_months is -5.2, below the approved floor of 0.0"
120 | - YES "the contract now sits below its approved floor"
121 | - NOT "conviction is 2 out of 5"
122 | - YES "the desk's conviction in this driver is weak"
123 | 
124 | Reply with JSON only, no prose and no code fences:
125 | {"label": "<one label>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "rationale": "<two or three sentences, no digits>"}"""
126 |

154 | owns every figure and renders them beside your text. You do not know the numbers, so you \

169 | 
170 | def _user_prompt(card: BeliefCard, breach: Breach, context: dict) -> str:
171 |     # Numbers go IN (the model needs the reading to judge it) but never come back out.
172 |     parts = [
173 |         f"STANDING BELIEF ({card.card_id}, version {card.version})",
174 |         f"  scope: {card.scope}",
175 |         f"  claim: {card.claim}",
176 |         f"  driver: {card.driver}",
177 |         f"  conviction in that driver: {card.confidence} out of 5",
178 |         f"  approved band for {card.metric}: {breach.expected_low} to {breach.expected_high}",
179 |         "",
180 |         "ENGINE READING",
181 |         f"  {breach.metric} is now {breach.observed}, which is {breach.direction} the band.",
182 |     ]
183 |     # Directions come from redline.as_directions: what moved, in words, never values.
184 |     # Giving the model the direction of every metric lets it reason about the full
185 |     # picture without any figure it could echo or do arithmetic on.
186 |     if context.get("directions"):
187 |         parts += ["", "WHAT MOVED (directions only — the engine owns every value)"]
188 |         parts += [context["directions"]]
189 |     if context.get("news"):
190 |         parts += ["", "EVIDENCE (verbatim, do not paraphrase as fact)"]
191 |         parts += [f"  - {snippet}" for snippet in context["news"]]
192 |     parts += ["", "Does this drift change the belief's story? Reply with the JSON object only."]
193 |     return "\n".join(parts)
194 | 
195 | 
196 | def _post(url: str, body: bytes, headers: dict, timeout: int) -> dict:
197 |     req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=body, headers=headers, method="POST")
198 |     with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
199 |         return json.loads(resp.read())
200 | 
201 | 
202 | def _extract_json(text: str) -> dict:
203 |     """Models wrap JSON in fences or chatter often enough to be worth one line of defense."""
204 |     start, end = text.find("{"), text.rfind("}")
205 |     if start == -1 or end <= start:
206 |         raise ValueError(f"no JSON object in model output: {text[:200]!r}")
207 |     return json.loads(text[start:end + 1])
